question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=eq:X filter where X matches a known consensus timestamp of a specific Hashgraph transaction, causing the staking rewards query to return only the reward associated with that exact transaction, confirming the existence and amount of a specific historical staking event?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply order=asc with token.id=gte:0 to cause the NFT history to be returned in ascending order from the beginning of Hashgraph history, allowing reconstruction of the complete chronological NFT acquisition history for any account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id=lte:X and serialnumber=lte:Y filter combination to cause getUpperFilters() to construct an upper-bound subquery, and by manipulating these bounds, cause the multi-union query to return a subset of NFT history that omits records above the attacker-chosen bounds, presenting an incomplete view of Hashgraph NFT history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a limit=0 or limit=1 parameter to the /rewards endpoint to cause the pagination link to be generated with the timestamp of the first reward, and by following the chain of next links, enumerate all historical staking reward consensus timestamps for any account in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp filter with a nanosecond-precision value (e.g., timestamp=eq:1234567890.123456789) that exactly matches a consensus timestamp in the staking_reward_transfer table, confirming the precise consensus timestamp of a historical Hashgraph staking event and leaking timing information about network activity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id=gte:0 filter to cause the NFT history query to include all NFTs with any spender, and by comparing results with and without the filter, determine which NFTs in an account's history have ever had an approved spender, leaking historical NFT approval records from Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EVM address that is a long-zero address (e.g., 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000001) to cause EntityService.getEncodedId() to resolve it to account 0.0.1, and then query the staking reward history of a system account, leaking sensitive Hashgraph history for privileged accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id=eq:X filter combined with a serialnumber=eq:Y filter to query for a specific NFT's history, and by iterating over serial numbers, reconstruct the complete historical ownership chain of a specific NFT token in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a limit parameter at the maximum allowed value combined with no other filters to cause NftService.getNfts() to return the maximum number of NFT records, and by following pagination links, enumerate the complete NFT ownership history for any account in Hashgraph without any rate limiting?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=gte:X filter where X is set to a consensus timestamp just before a known network upgrade, causing the staking rewards query to return only post-upgrade rewards, and by comparing with pre-upgrade results, infer the exact consensus timestamp of the network upgrade from Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted shard.realm.num account ID (e.g., 0.0.800) to query the staking reward account's own reward history, leaking the complete historical record of staking reward distributions from the Hashgraph staking reward account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id=gte:X and token.id=lte:X (same value, effectively eq) combined with serialnumber=gte:1 to cause the multi-union query to return all serial numbers for a specific token owned by an account, leaking the complete historical NFT serial number acquisition record for that token in Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id=lte:9999999999 filter to cause the NFT history query to include all NFTs with a spender ID up to the maximum, effectively returning all NFTs with any spender and leaking the complete historical spender approval record for an account in Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=gte:X filter with X set to 0 (epoch) to cause the staking rewards query to return all staking reward history from the beginning of Hashgraph, leaking the complete historical staking record for any account from genesis?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted EVM address that maps to a contract account rather than a user account, causing getNftsByAccountId() to return NFT history for a contract, leaking historical NFT holdings of smart contracts in Hashgraph without any type-based access control?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=lte:X filter where X is set to a consensus timestamp in the past, causing the staking rewards query to return only historical rewards before that timestamp, allowing the attacker to reconstruct the staking reward history of any account at any point in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=gte:X and timestamp=lte:Y range that spans a known staking period boundary, causing the staking rewards query to return rewards that straddle the period boundary, leaking information about the exact consensus timestamps of staking period transitions in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id=gte:1 and serialnumber=gte:1 filter combination to cause getLowerFilters() to produce a lower-bound subquery that starts from the very first NFT in Hashgraph history for an account, and by paginating through results, reconstruct the complete chronological NFT acquisition history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id=lte:X and serialnumber=lte:Y filter combination to cause getUpperFilters() to produce an upper-bound subquery, and by binary-searching over token.id and serialnumber values, determine the exact token.id and serial number of the most recently acquired NFT in an account's Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=eq:X filter with multiple repeated values (up to maxRepeatedQueryParameters) to cause the IN-clause to include only attacker-chosen consensus timestamps, causing the response to present a cherry-picked subset of staking reward history as if it were the complete record for those timestamps?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id=eq:X filter with multiple repeated values (routed to spenderIdInFilters) to cause the IN-clause to include only attacker-chosen spender IDs, filtering the NFT history to show only NFTs approved to specific spenders and hiding the rest of the historical approval record?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=gte:X filter where X is a nanosecond timestamp that falls between two consecutive consensus timestamps, causing the query to return rewards starting from the next consensus timestamp, and by binary-searching, determine the exact nanosecond consensus timestamps of all staking reward events in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an account ID that corresponds to a node account (e.g., 0.0.3) to query the staking reward history of a network node, leaking historical staking reward distributions to consensus nodes from Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id=eq:X filter where X is a token ID that was deleted, causing NftService.getNfts() to return NFT records with deleted=true, leaking historical information about deleted tokens and their NFT ownership history in Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id=gte:X and token.id=lte:Y range filter to cause the multi-union query to scan a range of token IDs in the NFT history, and by iterating over ranges, enumerate all token IDs for which an account has historical NFT records in Hashgraph?
